plan_summary: This 21-day intervention dismantles excessive social media use by reshaping identity, introducing discomfort, and creating irreversible changes in behavior and environment.

day_1.title: Track your scrolling patterns
day_1.description: Write down when and why you scroll social media. No pressure to change yet.

day_1.title: Identify your main trigger
day_1.description: Note the one situation or feeling that most often leads to scrolling.

day_1.title: Set your intention
day_1.description: Write one sentence about why reducing social media matters to you.

day_2.title: Remove apps from home screen
day_2.description: Move social media apps to a folder on the second screen or delete them.

day_2.title: Pause before opening
day_2.description: Before opening any social app, pause 30 seconds and name what you're feeling.

day_2.title: Choose an alternative
day_2.description: Pick a 5-minute healthy activity to try when the urge to scroll appears.

day_2.title: Set a daily limit
day_2.description: Use your phone's screen time settings to set a 30-minute daily limit.

day_3.title: Disable notifications
day_3.description: Turn off all social media notifications for 24 hours.

day_3.title: Practice urge surfing
day_3.description: When an urge to scroll appears, breathe deeply for 2 minutes and observe it.

day_3.title: Replace evening scrolling
day_3.description: Replace your usual evening scroll session with a 20-minute walk.

day_whys.day_1: These tasks help you understand your habit pattern without judgment, which is essential for creating lasting change.
day_whys.day_2: By adding friction and creating alternatives, you're making it harder to scroll automatically and easier to choose something better.
day_whys.day_3: Removing triggers and practicing urge management gives you control over your responses instead of reacting automatically.
